requirements.title: Self-registration with email, password, and unique username

bugs.description: Registration form (FE/src/features/auth/Register.tsx) and API (FE/src/api/auth.ts register()) accept only email and password. There is no separate Username field. The BE uses the default ASP.NET Identity /register endpoint (MapIdentityApi in Web/Program.cs) which sets UserName=Email. Therefore users cannot choose a distinct, unique username at registration as required.

requirements.title: Unique email, unique and immutable username, no email verification

bugs.description: Because username == email (see 2.1.1), the 'username unique and distinct from email' rule is only satisfied vacuously. There is no UI to set a username, so the immutability requirement is trivially met but the feature itself is missing.

requirements.title: Authentication: sign in, sign out, persistent login
Password reset and password change

bugs.description: Forgot password UI sends to the default Identity /forgotPassword endpoint which requires an email sender to be wired up; without SMTP configuration the reset link will not be delivered. The UI always shows 'Check your email for the reset link' even on success without verifying delivery.

requirements.title: Account deletion with cascading room/message/file cleanup
Presence states online/AFK/offline
AFK after 1 minute of inactivity
Multi-tab presence rules
Active sessions screen with browser/IP and per-session logout
Personal contact/friend list
Send friend request by username and from room user list; optional text

bugs.description: Friend requests can be sent by username from Contacts > Add Contact (FriendRequestDialog.tsx). However there is no 'Add friend' action exposed from the room members list in RoomMembersPanel.tsx, which violates 2.3.2 requiring request-from-room-user-list.

requirements.title: Friendship confirmation by recipient
Remove friend
User-to-user ban (blocks personal messaging, freezes history)

bugs.description: MessageService blocks sending in Direct rooms when a UserBlock exists, but there is no UI indication that a direct chat is 'frozen/read-only' on the other side. Existing history remains visible because it is not deleted, but the composer shows only a generic error after submit rather than a disabled/read-only state. Friend relationship termination on block is not verified in FriendService.
There is no visible 'Blocked users' list in the UI; unblock is not reachable through the Contacts page.

requirements.title: Personal messaging only between friends

bugs.description: openDirectChat(username) is called from FriendList only, but DirectChatService.cs does not verify in the BE that the two users are mutual friends before creating/opening the direct room. Two strangers could still converse if a direct room already exists or is created via API.

requirements.title: Any user may create a chat room
Chat room properties (name, description, visibility, owner, admins, members, bans); unique names
Public room catalog with search and free join
Private rooms hidden from catalog; join only by invitation
Join/leave; owner cannot leave (must delete)
Room deletion removes messages, files, images
Owner/admin roles and permissions matrix
Removing member acts as ban; lose access to messages and files

bugs.description: In ManageRoomDialog, the Members panel has both 'Remove' and 'Ban' buttons, but the requirement says 'removing a user is treated as a ban'. Having two separate actions lets an admin 'Remove' a user who could then rejoin a public room freely, contradicting 2.4.8.

requirements.title: Users may invite others to private rooms
Personal and room chats identical in UI/features; personal chats have no admins
Message content: text, multiline, emoji, attachments, replies; 3KB limit; UTF-8
Message replies visually outlined/quoted

bugs.description: ReplyToMessageId is stored on the backend and sent when composing, but MessageItem.tsx does not render a quoted block showing the replied-to message's author/snippet. The rendered message never references the original. The MessageSchema on the FE lacks fields like replyToText/replyToAuthorUserName, and the API does not include them. This violates 2.5.3.

requirements.title: Edit own messages; 'edited' indicator
Message deletion by author or room admin
Persistent chronological history; infinite scroll; offline delivery
Images and arbitrary files
Upload via button and paste
Preserve original file name; optional attachment comment

bugs.description: The composer passes the draft text as attachment 'comment' on upload, but there is no explicit, dedicated UI field to provide an attachment comment (no prompt/input tied to the pick action). The wireframe shows a distinct 'comment:' field under the attachment card.

requirements.title: Access control on attachments tied to room membership
Files persist after uploader loses access
Unread indicators on rooms and personal dialogs
Low-latency presence updates
General layout: top menu, center messages, bottom input, sidebar
Right side layout: rooms/contacts list collapses; right-side members

bugs.description: RightSidebar.tsx has Rooms, Invitations, and Direct Messages collapsibles but does NOT include a Contacts (friends) list in the sidebar, contrary to 4.1.1 and the wireframes which show CONTACTS inline in the side panel with presence and unread counts.

requirements.title: Chat window autoscroll/infinite scroll behavior
Composer: multiline, emoji, attachments, reply
Unread badges near room and contact names

bugs.description: Unread badges are shown next to rooms and next to direct-message conversations, but because there is no separate Contacts list in the sidebar (see 4.1.1), per-contact unread indicators on the contact list itself are missing.

requirements.title: Admin UI via menus and modal dialogs (ban/unban, remove, admins, delete)
Advanced: Jabber/XMPP support and federation

bugs.description: No Jabber/XMPP module, federation logic, admin dashboard, or federation statistics page found anywhere in BE or FE. Advanced requirement is optional and not implemented.

wireframes.screen_name: Landing with Sign In / Register / Forgot password panels

bugs.description: Wireframe Register panel includes: Email, Username, Password, Confirm password. The implemented Register.tsx has only Email, Password, Confirm password. The Username field is missing from the form and from the backing API (see requirement 2.1.1).

There is no unified landing page with 'LOGO [Sign in] [Register]' header; /login, /register, /forgot are separate self-contained card pages. Acceptable functionally but cosmetically different from the wireframe.

wireframes.screen_name: Main chat screen (TopNav, right rooms/contacts sidebar, center chat, members panel, composer)

bugs.description: Right sidebar shows Rooms, Invitations and Direct Messages, but the 'CONTACTS' section with presence dots and unread counts from the wireframe is absent in RightSidebar.tsx.
Wireframe splits rooms into 'Public Rooms' and 'Private Rooms' sub-accordions within the sidebar; the implementation lumps all group rooms into a single 'Rooms' collapsible.

Reply-quote rendering missing: wireframe shows '[10:25] Carol replied to Bob: > Hello team\n Can we make this private?'. MessageItem.tsx does not render the quoted original message above the reply body.

Attachment card in wireframe shows 'comment:' row beneath the file name. Attachments are shown with an optional comment only when present, and there is no dedicated UI to add a comment when uploading; paste/pick flow reuses composer text as the comment.
Composer layout matches wireframe closely, but the 'Replying to: Bob x' chip doesn't show the replied-to user's name, only the literal text 'Replying to message'.

wireframes.screen_name: Manage Room modal with tabs Members/Admins/Banned users/Invitations/Settings

bugs.description: Wireframe defines FIVE tabs: Members, Admins, Banned users, Invitations, Settings. ManageRoomDialog.tsx implements only FOUR: Members, Admins, Banned, Settings. There is no 'Invitations' tab; invitations are only reachable via an ad-hoc 'Invite user' button + window.prompt in the header.

Banned users tab in the wireframe shows columns 'Username / Banned by / Date/time / Actions'. Implementation shows only username and reason, missing 'Banned by' and 'Date/time' columns, in violation of 2.4.7 ('view who banned each banned user').
Members tab lacks 'Status' (online/AFK/offline) and 'Search member' filter shown in the wireframe; the implemented list has neither presence indicators nor a search input.

Members tab of the implementation exposes separate 'Ban' and 'Remove' actions. Per requirement 2.4.8 a remove is defined as a ban; the wireframe shows 'Ban' and 'Remove from room' as distinct actions, creating ambiguity, but the app then lets admins remove without banning, allowing rejoin of public rooms. This creates a reliability defect against 3.6 consistency of room bans.

Admins tab in the wireframe includes explicit note 'alice = owner (cannot lose admin rights)'. Implementation only disables the Remove button by omission; it does not label the owner or show the rule.

all_bugs.description: Registration form has no Username field; backend uses default Identity endpoint that sets UserName=Email. Users cannot choose a distinct unique username as required by 2.1.1.
Reply-quote rendering missing in MessageItem.tsx: replyToMessageId is stored but the replied-to message is not shown above the new message body.
Register wireframe requires Username field; implemented Register.tsx omits it.
Reply-quote rendering is absent in the message list, contrary to the main chat wireframe.
Manage Room modal is missing the Invitations tab shown in the wireframe; invitations use an ad-hoc window.prompt instead.
Banned users tab lacks 'Banned by' and 'Date/time' columns required by the wireframe and by 2.4.7 ('view who banned each banned user').
Members panel offers both 'Remove' and 'Ban'; removing does not create a room ban, so removed users can rejoin public rooms, violating 2.4.8 and 3.6 consistency.
Same removal-vs-ban ambiguity surfaces in the requirement verification.
No distinct Username concept: usernames equal emails, making the '2.1.2 unique username distinct from email' rule vacuous.
No way to add a friend from the room members list (RoomMembersPanel has no 'Add friend' action).
Direct chat composer does not render a frozen/read-only state when the other user is blocked; only a post-submit error is shown.
DirectChatService does not validate that two users are mutual friends before opening a direct chat.
Attachment comment has no dedicated input; composer draft text is reused, contrary to 2.6.3 wording and wireframe layout.
Right sidebar lacks a Contacts list with presence/unread badges (4.1.1 and main-chat wireframe).
Right sidebar does not split rooms into Public/Private accordions as shown in the wireframe.
Attachment comment field is missing from the upload UI per wireframe.
Members tab has no Status column (online/AFK/offline) and no member search; both appear in the wireframe.
Admins tab does not display the 'owner cannot lose admin rights' clarification from the wireframe.
Forgot-password flow depends on SMTP; UI always claims success without verification.
No Blocked users list/unblock UI reachable from Contacts.
Per-contact unread badges missing because Contacts list is absent from the sidebar.
Reply chip in composer shows literal 'Replying to message' instead of the replied-to user's name as in the wireframe.
No Jabber/XMPP dashboard or federation statistics implemented (optional advanced requirement 6).
Auth pages are separate card pages rather than the LOGO + [Sign in] [Register] top-bar landing in the wireframe.
